definition: Service Level Agreement — a contract clause defining minimum performance standards a vendor must meet.
Total Cost of Ownership — the full cost of a product over its lifecycle, including hidden and indirect costs.
Statement of Work — a document defining the specific tasks, deliverables, and timeline a vendor agrees to.
A security compliance framework that audits how a company protects customer data across five trust principles.
General Data Protection Regulation — EU law governing how companies collect, store, and use personal data.
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — US law requiring safeguards for protected health information.
An international standard for managing information security through systematic risk management.
Single Sign-On — an authentication method that lets users log in to multiple systems with one set of credentials.
A contract clause where one party agrees to cover the other's losses from specific claims or damages.
The timeframe after delivery during which a vendor must fix defects or issues at no extra cost.
The state of meeting required legal, regulatory, or contractual standards and obligations.
The percentage of time a service is operational and available, typically measured over a month or year.
How long a vendor stores your data after the contract ends, and what happens to it afterward.
A clause that automatically extends a contract for another term unless you cancel before the deadline.
A contractual limit on how much a vendor can increase prices during the term or at renewal.
An extra charge when usage exceeds the limits included in your contract or subscription tier.
A billing model where you pay a fixed amount for each individual user who has access to the service.
A billing model where you only pay for users who actually logged in or used the service during a period.
The scheduled date when a vendor's solution becomes fully operational in your environment.
The number of working days from contract signing until the vendor delivers the agreed solution.
Pre-agreed financial penalties a vendor pays if they fail to meet specific contract obligations.
A clause excusing a party from contract obligations due to extraordinary events beyond their control.
An arrangement where source code or assets are held by a third party and released if the vendor fails.
Software as a Service — software delivered over the internet on a subscription basis, hosted by the vendor.
Request for Proposal — a formal document asking vendors to submit proposals for a defined project or need.
Non-Disclosure Agreement — a contract preventing parties from sharing confidential information.
Master Service Agreement — the overarching contract governing the general terms of a vendor relationship.
Key Performance Indicator — a measurable value used to evaluate how well a vendor meets objectives.
A clause allowing either party to end the contract without cause, usually with advance notice.
A third-party company that a vendor uses to process your data on their behalf.
